name: Stage 1: Vielle-Aure to La Mongie — Grand Raid des Pyrénées
name_original: Étape 1 : De Vielle-Aure à La Mongie — Grand Raid des Pyrénées en off
description:
This first stage from Vielle-Aure is the shortest of your adventure. Its 30.9 kilometers are mostly in elevation gain in order to cross several mountain passes while meandering among many lakes.

The first flat kilometers towards Saint-Lary allow you to warm up before tackling a first climb of around seven kilometers above the village and its valley. Then, a slight flat area allows you to catch your breath before climbing to the Col de Portet. From there, a slight descent takes you to the heights of the superb Lac de l'Oule.

A long false flat gradually takes you up to the Col de Bastanet while discovering the magnificent Bastan lakes. In the middle is the refuge of the same name which invites you to take a nice break in a sublime setting. Take the opportunity to recharge your water and possibly eat a small snack before completing your climb to the pass. Perched between the peaks of Bastan and Portarras, it reveals a magnificent panorama of the various lakes below that you are about to discover.

The lakes of Hourquette, Campana and Gréziolles follow one another in a descent which extends over almost seven kilometres. Finally you have a short climb, followed by the same distance downhill before reaching the ski resort of La Mongie which marks the end of your stage.
region_intro: Jogging around Vielle-Aure offers access to diverse terrain within the Hautes-Pyrénées region of France. The area is characterized by the preserved Aure Valley, bordering the Pyrenees National Park and the Néouvielle Nature Reserve, which features over 70 high-altitude lakes. Joggers can explore routes along the Neste river, through pastoral landscapes, and into mountainous areas with significant elevation changes. This natural setting provides varied running experiences from gentle valley paths to challenging mountain trails. Best jogging routes around Vielle-Aure The most popular jogging route is Stage 1: Vielle-Aure to La Mongie — Grand Raid des Pyrénées, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 40 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain through mountain landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Col de Bastanet and Pic de Bastan Trail, a difficult 10.3 miles (16.5 km) path. This route offers views of high-altitude lakes and mountain peaks within the Pyrenees. Local runners also love the Vielle-Aure loop from Vielle-Aure, a 8.2 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through the local valley and surrounding hills, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Jogging around Vielle-Aure is defined by mountain valleys, high-altitude lakes, and river paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle riverside jogs to demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Vielle-Aure are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1900 runners have used komoot to explore Vielle-Aure's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes around Vielle-Aure?
A: Jogging routes around Vielle-Aure offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find gentle paths along the Neste river, pastoral landscapes in valleys like the Géla, and more challenging mountainous areas with significant elevation changes, especially as you approach the Pyrenees National Park and the Néouvielle Nature Reserve. The region is characterized by mountain valleys, high-altitude lakes, and river paths, providing options from easy riverside jogs to demanding mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Vielle-Aure?
A: While many routes in the Vielle-Aure area are considered challenging, there are options suitable for beginners or those seeking a less strenuous run. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20526952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vielle-Aure – Path Along the Neste River loop from Guchan&lt;/a&gt; offers a relatively flat 8.1 km (5 miles) path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a peaceful jog through fields and past rural villages.

Q: What are some scenic points or natural features I can see while running near Vielle-Aure?
A: The Vielle-Aure region is rich in natural beauty. Many running routes offer stunning views of the Pyrenees National Park and the Néouvielle Nature Reserve. You can encounter over 70 high-altitude lakes, including notable ones like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3755161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Orédon&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Aubert&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/609892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Aumar&lt;/a&gt;. The Rioumajou and Moudang valleys, classified as Natura 2000 sites, also provide picturesque landscapes with diverse flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in the Vielle-Aure area?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20527613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vielle-Aure loop from Vielle-Aure&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.2-mile (13.3 km) trail that takes you through the local valley and surrounding hills. Another circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38642399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastan Refuge – Lac de l'Oule loop from Aragnouet&lt;/a&gt;, which is a longer 17.9 km (11.1 miles) run offering beautiful mountain scenery.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Vielle-Aure?
A: The running routes around Vielle-Aure are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the breathtaking mountain backdrops, the variety of terrain from gentle river paths to challenging ascents, and the access to pristine natural areas like the Pyrenees National Park and the Néouvielle Nature Reserve. The stunning high-altitude lakes are also frequently highlighted as a major draw.

Q: Can I find running routes that are suitable for families in Vielle-Aure?
A: For families, routes along the Neste river or the marked 'Chemin des Traques' offer pleasant and less strenuous options. These trails provide peaceful environments through fields and between villages, avoiding the significant elevation changes found on more mountainous routes. The Agos leisure base in Vielle-Aure also features walking paths that are suitable for a family jog.

Q: Is Vielle-Aure a good place for advanced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. Vielle-Aure is an excellent destination for advanced trail runners. The region offers numerous challenging routes with significant elevation gains, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030612598/stage-1-vielle-aure-to-la-mongie-grand-raid-des-pyrenees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stage 1: Vielle-Aure to La Mongie — Grand Raid des Pyrénées&lt;/a&gt;, a 19.2-mile (30.9 km) trail with over 2,200 meters of ascent. The presence of mountain peaks like Arbizon and legendary passes like Col du Tourmalet provides exhilarating terrain for experienced runners seeking demanding ascents and technical descents.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Vielle-Aure?
A: The best time for running in Vielle-Aure largely depends on your preference for terrain and weather. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for exploring the valleys and lower mountain trails. Summer is excellent for accessing high-altitude routes and lakes in the Néouvielle Nature Reserve. While winter offers unique snowy landscapes, many higher trails may be inaccessible or require specialized gear due to snow and ice.

Q: Are there any specific parking facilities for accessing running trails in Vielle-Aure?
A: While specific parking facilities for each trail are not detailed, Vielle-Aure and surrounding villages generally offer public parking areas. For routes accessing the Néouvielle Nature Reserve, designated parking might be available near popular starting points like Lacs d'Orédon or Lac de l'Oule. It's advisable to check local signage or specific route descriptions for parking information at trailheads.

Q: Can I reach running trailheads in Vielle-Aure using public transport?
A: During the winter season, free shuttle buses operate between Saint-Lary, Vignec, Vielle-Aure, and Bourisp, which can help access various starting points for outdoor activities. For other times of the year, public transport options might be more limited, and accessing some remote trailheads may require a car. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for the specific season you plan to visit.

Q: Are there any trails that feature high-altitude lakes in the Vielle-Aure area?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its high-altitude lakes, especially within the Néouvielle Nature Reserve. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880496677/col-de-bastanet-and-pic-de-bastan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Bastanet and Pic de Bastan Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer views of these stunning lakes. The reserve boasts over 70 lakes, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3755161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Orédon&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Aubert&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/609892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Aumar&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque backdrops for your run.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of running routes around Vielle-Aure?
A: The running routes around Vielle-Aure cater to a wide range of abilities, though the majority tend to be on the more challenging side. Out of 33 available routes, 28 are classified as 'difficult', 2 as 'moderate', and 3 as 'easy'. This indicates a strong prevalence of trails with significant elevation changes and technical terrain, particularly in the mountainous areas bordering the Pyrenees National Park.
tips:
Culminating at 2,215 meters above sea level, it is the highest pass in the Pyrenees taken by a road.
Overlooking the village of Saint-Lary Soulan, it offers a lovely panorama over the valley.

The Bastan refuge is located a little higher up, on the shores of Lac du Milieu. It is open from the last weekend of May to the first weekend of October, continuously.

It offers a 12-seat dormitory as well as a refectory for which we advise you to book in advance on the refuge website: http://refugedebastan.fr/dates-douverture/

However, the refuge remains open out of season (you will need to bring your duvets) to accommodate hikers in need of shelter.
The Col de Bastanet is 2,509 meters above sea level. It offers a splendid view of the various Bastan lakes.
Lake Gréziolles is a superb lake perched at an altitude of 2,113 meters. Raised by a dam, it is home to many species of fish including brown trout.
La Mongie is a winter sports resort in the French Pyrenees which offers many accommodation and restaurant options.
